text:
Piers Morganhas publiclyadmitted to being wrongabout Simone Biles after the 27-year-old athlete became the most decorated gymnast in history.

In a 2021MailOnlinecolumn, the broadcasterbrutally criticised the championforwithdrawing from the Tokyo Olympicsfor mental health reasons andwas met with backlash.

At the time, she was due to take part in all four parts of the women's team final gymnastics competition as the leader of Team USA.

In his scathing article, he wrote: “The world's greatest ever gymnast, a woman who proudly told me how she fought back after making big mistakes to win Gold, just gave up at the first hurdle of these Olympics.

“She left her team to fight on without their leader and supreme motivational champion, and rather than win the Gold medal they were hot favourites to win, they came second to the Russians.”

Read more:Piers Morgan's eight-word response to BBC knowing about Huw Edwards' arrest



In the brutal take-down, he continued: “You're not just at these Games for yourself, Simone.

“You are part of Team USA, representing the United States of America, and hundreds of millions of American people watching back home, not to mention all the sponsors who've paid huge sums to support you.

“And when you quit, you were performing as part of a gymnastics team, not yourself.”

Piers’ harsh criticism continued on Twitter, where he wrote: “Are 'mental health issues' now the go-to excuse for any poor performance in elite sport? What a joke.

Don't miss:Simone Biles claims sixth Olympic gold in gripping gymnastics all-around final

Don't miss:Simone Biles' husband makes touching sacrifice to watch her go for Olympic gold

Don't miss:GMB's Ed Balls slammed by fans for 'car crash' Jeremy Hunt interview



We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

“Just admit you did badly, made mistakes, and will strive to do better next time. Kids need strong role models not this nonsense.”

He then advised Simone: “You can either listen to snowflake Twitter, @Simone_Biles - or listen to me.

“You're a great champion, and great champions get back on their feet when they get knocked down.

"So, re-engage in these Games, win Gold, and inspire with the power of resilience not resignation. Go for it.”



However, since Simone’s incredible wins at the 2024 Paris Olympics, Piers has since apologised to the athlete and taken back his comments.

Alongside a photo of them together from their 2019 interview, Piers took toInstagramand penned: “Congrats @simonebiles on becoming the most decorated American gymnast of all time.

“I was too harsh on you in 2021 when you quit the team finals,” he admitted.

“You’re a great champion, and have since proved me wrong in the best possible way - by letting your supreme talent do the talking.”

title: Piers Morgan’s scathing comments about Simone Biles before U-turn | Celebrity News | Showbiz & TV | Express.co.uk
author: Emmanuella Ngimbi
description: Piers Morgan has retracted his 2021 remarks about the decorated American gymnast.
